_meta.purpose: Deterministic, KEYLESS, LIST-BASED classification for the two THEMATIC instruments (EU Open Source Strategy -> 'Open source', Strategic Roadmap for Digitalisation & AI in Energy -> 'AI in energy'). A company is classified iff it appears on a roster's members[]. No model calls. Used by scripts/classify_theme.py.
_meta.why_lists: Membership is the credibility line: 'included because it is a smartEn member / IPCEI participant' is a citable, falsifiable claim — not a heuristic guess.
_meta.tag_vs_primary: Membership does NOT auto-recategorize. sync_universe.py resolves the PRIMARY category: (1) if the company classifies into a SECTOR instrument (Semiconductors/Cloud & AI) by codes+keywords, that sector stays PRIMARY and the roster becomes a secondary tag in affiliations[]; (2) a member with no sector hit gets the thematic category as PRIMARY, UNLESS market_cap_eur > CAP_EUR (default 20e9, env CAP_EUR) -> demoted to tag-only (a large diversified member is not a pure-play theme); (3) private/foundation members (<=cap or unpriced) -> thematic primary as a static non-listed entry.
_meta.record_fields: auto-theme entries set source='auto-theme', confidence=1.0, rationale='Member of <roster>'; affiliations[] carries the roster tag(s) on ANY record (curated, auto-sector, or auto-theme).
_meta.DATA_SOURCE_STATUS_2026-06-08: PROBED — none of the chosen public member directories are keyless-parseable via plain HTTP, so auto-fetch is DISABLED for all rosters and members[] are maintainer-curated (mode:'manual'). classify_theme.py logs this every run; it does NOT scrape JS or invent members. Findings: smartEn (members rendered client-side via JS templates {{data.display_name}}); OpenForum Europe (no member names in static HTML); Eclipse Foundation (JS shell; membership API redirects to API docs, no keyless member JSON). NEEDS-DECISION: how to source verified thematic membership — (a) maintainer-curated members[] transcribed from the cited directories / EC IPCEI press releases (static, sourced, dated, reviewed); (b) reverse-engineer a site's members AJAX/JSON endpoint (brittle); (c) drop thematic auto-discovery and keep thematic companies purely curated. members[] are intentionally EMPTY until this is decided — the dry run reports 0 thematic members rather than faking them.

disabled_rosters.disabled: HTTP 404 — no EU-scoped members directory exists (only the global members.linuxfoundation.org, not EU/EEA-filterable). Dropped 2026-06-08.

rosters.autofetch_reason: JS-rendered shell; no keyless member JSON (api.eclipse.org/membership/members redirects to API docs).
Member names not present in static HTML (rendered/asset-based).

rosters.scope: EU/EEA demand-side flexibility, DER and smart-grid software members
rosters.autofetch_reason: Members rendered client-side via JS templates ({{data.display_name}}); not in static HTML.

rosters.scope: EU/EEA companies named as IPCEI participants touching energy digitalisation
rosters.autofetch_reason: Participant names are in EC press-release prose/PDF, not a structured keyless list.

rosters.list: EU Digitalisation & AI in Energy roadmap — named organisations
rosters.scope: organisations explicitly named in the roadmap / its annexes
rosters.autofetch_reason: Names are in roadmap prose/annex, not a structured keyless list.

AI in energy.notes: ENTSO-E membership is national TSOs (not listed software vendors) — deliberately not used; smartEn is the right software/flexibility roster.
